claims.claim_text: A payments company experienced a regional database failover incident during a cloud networking event, with read replica lag and untested failover under transaction load.
claims.span_hint: section: Why This Module Matters, first paragraph
claims.anchor_text: A payments company had a database failover plan that looked excellent in a review document and frightening during the incident. The primary region became unstable during a cloud networking event, the read replica lagged behind the write leader, and the team discovered that the failover checklist had never been tested under real transaction load. One engineer watched customer checkouts time out while another engineer tried to decide whether the replica was fresh enough to promote. The system had backups, dashboards, runbooks, and senior people online, but it still depended on humans making high-pressure decisions while the business was losing money.
claims.suggested_rewrite: A single-primary database failover plan can look excellent in a review document and frightening during an incident. If the primary region becomes unstable, a replica is behind, and the failover checklist has never been tested under real transaction load, the team may have to make high-pressure promotion decisions while users are already affected. Backups, dashboards, runbooks, and experienced engineers help, but they do not remove the risk of discovering failover gaps during the incident itself.
claims.rationale: The anecdote is presented as a real incident but names no citable source; hard-rule war stories cannot be softened, so the rewrite removes unverifiable specifics.

claims.claim_text: CockroachDB supports the PostgreSQL wire protocol and PostgreSQL client drivers.
claims.span_hint: section: The Mental Model, first paragraph
claims.rationale: The CockroachDB design document directly states support for the PostgreSQL wire protocol.

claims.claim_text: CockroachDB stores data in key ranges; ranges split as data grows; ranges have replicas and a leaseholder/Raft leader for coordination.
claims.span_hint: section: The Mental Model, paragraph after request-path diagram
claims.rationale: Cockroach Labs documentation is the primary source for this product behavior, but cockroachlabs.com is not on the trusted-domain allowlist.

claims.claim_text: Monotonically increasing primary keys can concentrate writes at the end of an index, while high-cardinality UUID keys often distribute writes more evenly.
claims.span_hint: section: The Mental Model, paragraph beginning 'This distinction explains'
claims.rationale: This is a CockroachDB schema and workload distribution claim that should cite CockroachDB schema-design guidance, which is off-allowlist.

claims.claim_text: CockroachDB uses serializable isolation by default, and conflicting transactions may require retries.
claims.span_hint: section: The Mental Model, paragraph before SQL schema
claims.rationale: The claim is directly supported by CockroachDB transaction documentation, but the host is not allowlisted.

claims.claim_text: Raft requires a quorum for writes; with three replicas, two replicas form a quorum.
claims.span_hint: section: Ranges, Raft, and Failure Behavior, first paragraph
claims.rationale: The Raft paper is the primary reference for majority consensus/quorum behavior.

claims.claim_text: CockroachDB uses separate Raft groups per range, so failures and leadership movement are granular by range rather than global.
claims.span_hint: section: Ranges, Raft, and Failure Behavior, paragraph after one-range diagram
claims.rationale: The CockroachDB design document describes ranges replicated by Raft and the layered architecture.

claims.claim_text: CockroachDB can only tolerate failures that replica placement actually separates across nodes, zones, or storage failure domains.
claims.span_hint: section: Ranges, Raft, and Failure Behavior, paragraph beginning 'The dangerous misunderstanding'
claims.rationale: This product-specific topology guidance should cite CockroachDB topology documentation, which is off-allowlist.

claims.claim_text: CockroachDB exposes unavailable and under-replicated range counts through crdb_internal.kv_store_status.
claims.span_hint: section: Ranges, Raft, and Failure Behavior, SQL query and following paragraph
claims.rationale: The internal table and columns are CockroachDB-specific and require CockroachDB documentation, whose host is off-allowlist.

claims.claim_text: CockroachDB on Kubernetes can be deployed with a Helm chart or the CockroachDB operator.
claims.span_hint: section: Deploying CockroachDB on Kubernetes, first paragraph and deployment-options table
claims.rationale: This deployment capability is documented by Cockroach Labs, but cockroachlabs.com is not allowlisted.

claims.claim_text: Kubernetes StatefulSets provide stable pod identity, stable network identity, and stable storage through volume claim templates.
claims.span_hint: section: Deploying CockroachDB on Kubernetes, paragraph after rollout commands
claims.rationale: The Kubernetes StatefulSet documentation directly covers stable identities and persistent storage.

claims.claim_text: Insecure CockroachDB mode is acceptable only for disposable labs; production should use TLS, protected users, and restricted network access.
claims.span_hint: section: Deploying CockroachDB on Kubernetes, paragraph beginning 'The example uses insecure mode'
claims.rationale: This is a security claim about CockroachDB deployment mode and should cite CockroachDB security documentation, currently off-allowlist.

claims.claim_text: Kubernetes pod anti-affinity and topology spread constraints can separate pods by hostname and spread them across topology domains such as zones.
claims.span_hint: section: Deploying CockroachDB on Kubernetes, scheduling-intent paragraph and YAML fragment
claims.rationale: Kubernetes documentation directly describes topology spread constraints and their topology keys.

claims.claim_text: CockroachDB multi-region features can improve local reads but may increase write latency because quorum can cross geographic distance.
claims.span_hint: section: Locality and Multi-Region Design, paragraph beginning 'The most common beginner mistake'
claims.rationale: The claim is specific to CockroachDB multi-region behavior and should cite CockroachDB docs, which are off-allowlist.

claims.claim_text: ALTER DATABASE ... SURVIVE REGION FAILURE configures a CockroachDB database to tolerate a region failure, assuming surviving regions can form quorum.
claims.span_hint: section: Locality and Multi-Region Design, SQL block and following paragraph
claims.rationale: CockroachDB survival-goal semantics are documented by Cockroach Labs, an off-allowlist host.

claims.claim_text: REGIONAL BY ROW places rows according to region metadata and supports locality-aware access patterns, but it is not a complete compliance control.
claims.span_hint: section: Locality and Multi-Region Design, paragraph before REGIONAL BY ROW SQL
claims.rationale: The row-locality behavior is a CockroachDB capability and requires CockroachDB primary documentation, which is not allowlisted.

claims.claim_text: CockroachDB EXPLAIN can be used to inspect whether a query uses locality-aware filters and indexes.
claims.span_hint: section: Locality and Multi-Region Design, paragraph after EXPLAIN SQL
claims.rationale: This cites a CockroachDB SQL planning feature documented on the off-allowlist Cockroach Labs docs site.

claims.claim_text: CockroachDB supports BACKUP DATABASE, SHOW BACKUPS, nodelocal backup destinations, and production backup destinations such as S3, GCS, or Azure Blob.
claims.span_hint: section: Worked Example, backup decision flow and Step one
claims.rationale: CockroachDB backup capabilities and storage destinations require CockroachDB backup documentation, which is off-allowlist.

claims.claim_text: CockroachDB supports recurring backup schedules created with CREATE SCHEDULE FOR BACKUP and inspected with SHOW SCHEDULES.
claims.span_hint: section: Worked Example, Step two and Step three
claims.rationale: The exact scheduling syntax and SHOW SCHEDULES behavior are documented by Cockroach Labs, whose host is off-allowlist.

claims.claim_text: CockroachDB can restore a database from a backup into a separate database name using RESTORE DATABASE ... WITH new_db_name.
claims.span_hint: section: Worked Example, Step four
claims.rationale: The RESTORE syntax and new_db_name option are CockroachDB-specific and documented on an off-allowlist primary source.

claims.claim_text: CockroachDB changefeeds stream row changes to supported sinks, including Kafka and cloud storage, and sinkless changefeeds can be used for learning.
claims.span_hint: section: Worked Example, Step five and following paragraph
claims.rationale: Changefeed behavior and sink support are CockroachDB product capabilities documented by Cockroach Labs, not currently allowlisted.

claims.claim_text: A changefeed is not a replacement for backups because incorrect writes can be streamed downstream just like correct writes.
claims.span_hint: section: Worked Example, paragraph after CREATE CHANGEFEED
claims.rationale: This recoverability claim should be anchored in CockroachDB backup/changefeed guidance; the relevant primary docs are off-allowlist.

claims.claim_text: CockroachDB exposes SQL-level introspection, an Admin UI, Prometheus metrics, and a /_status/vars endpoint.
claims.span_hint: section: Monitoring, Hot Ranges, and Operational Signals, first paragraph and ServiceMonitor YAML
claims.rationale: Monitoring endpoints and metric behavior are CockroachDB-specific capabilities documented off-allowlist.

claims.claim_text: CockroachDB exposes hot range information through crdb_internal.kv_hot_ranges.
claims.span_hint: section: Monitoring, Hot Ranges, and Operational Signals, hot ranges SQL query
claims.rationale: The internal hot-range table is a CockroachDB-specific diagnostic interface requiring CockroachDB docs, whose host is off-allowlist.

claims.claim_text: Hot ranges can result from many clients updating the same row, inserting into a narrow key range, or using an index that concentrates work on one range.
claims.span_hint: section: Monitoring, Hot Ranges, and Operational Signals, paragraph beginning 'A hot range happens'
claims.rationale: This is CockroachDB hotspot diagnosis guidance and should cite CockroachDB primary documentation, currently off-allowlist.

claims.claim_text: kind supports multi-node disposable Kubernetes clusters and extraMounts for mounting host directories into nodes.
claims.span_hint: section: Hands-On Exercise, Step 1
claims.rationale: The kind configuration behavior is real and documented by kind, but kind.sigs.k8s.io is not on the allowlist.

claims.claim_text: CockroachDB's --external-io-dir setting is required for the nodelocal backup destination used in the lab.
claims.span_hint: section: Hands-On Exercise, Step 2
claims.rationale: The nodelocal and external I/O directory behavior is CockroachDB-specific and needs CockroachDB documentation, which is off-allowlist.

further_reading.title: CockroachDB Design
further_reading.why_relevant: Explains CockroachDB's layered architecture, PostgreSQL wire protocol support, ranges, and Raft replication.

further_reading.title: In Search of an Understandable Consensus Algorithm
further_reading.why_relevant: Primary Raft paper for understanding quorum-based replication and consensus behavior.

further_reading.title: Kubernetes StatefulSets
further_reading.why_relevant: Covers the stable identity and storage primitives used for stateful database workloads on Kubernetes.

further_reading.title: Kubernetes Pod Topology Spread Constraints
further_reading.why_relevant: Documents the scheduling controls used to spread database pods across topology domains.

notes: Most CockroachDB primary documentation lives on cockroachlabs.com, which is not in the current allowlist. Those entries are marked needs_allowlist_expansion rather than weak_anchor because the claims are product-specific and senior-tier citation quality requires primary docs.
